title: Lesson 43 – Appearance and Change
description: Aussehen und Veraenderung: ～そうです (sieht aus wie / scheint), ～てきます (hingehen und zurueckkommen / beginnen), ～ていきます (von jetzt an weiter).

vocabulary.meaning: to break, to snap, to run out
to come off, to be removed
to run out, to be gone

grammar.title: V-masu stem / Adj stem + そうです — Appearance (looks like ~)
grammar.structure: V-masu stem + そうです / い-Adj (drop い) + そうです / な-Adj (drop な) + そうです
grammar.explanation: ～そうです expresses the speaker's impression or conjecture based on what they see. It means 'it looks like ~' or 'it seems ~'. For verbs, remove ます and add そうです. For い-adjectives, remove the final い and add そうです. For な-adjectives, remove な and add そうです. Exception: いい becomes よさそうです, ない becomes なさそうです. This form expresses the speaker's own visual impression — not hearsay.
grammar.example_sentences:
① このケーキは おいしそうですね。
  (Kono keeki wa oishisou desu ne.)
  — This cake looks delicious, doesn't it?
② 雨が 降りそうです。
  (Ame ga furisou desu.)
  — It looks like it's going to rain.
③ この本は おもしろそうです。
  (Kono hon wa omoshirosou desu.)
  — This book looks interesting.
④ 彼は 元気そうです。
  (Kare wa genkisou desu.)
  — He looks well/healthy.

grammar.title: ～てきます — Go and come back / Begin to
grammar.explanation: ～てきます has two main uses: (1) To go somewhere, do something, and come back. This implies the speaker will return. (2) To describe a change that has started and is continuing toward the present. It indicates something has begun to happen and the effect is approaching or becoming noticeable.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑤ ちょっと コーヒーを 買ってきます。
  (Chotto koohii o katte kimasu.)
  — I'll go buy some coffee (and come back).
⑥ だんだん 暖かく なってきましたね。
  (Dandan atatakakunatte kimashita ne.)
  — It has gradually become warmer, hasn't it?
⑦ 人口が 増えてきました。
  (Jinkou ga fuete kimashita.)
  — The population has been increasing.

grammar.title: ～ていきます — Continue from now on / Go away
grammar.explanation: ～ていきます is the counterpart of ～てきます. It has two main uses: (1) To do something and then go (leave). (2) To describe a change that will continue from now into the future, moving away from the present moment. It indicates something will keep happening going forward.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑧ お弁当を 持っていきます。
  (Obentou o motte ikimasu.)
  — I'll take a boxed lunch (with me).
⑨ これから 気温は 下がっていくでしょう。
  (Korekara kion wa sagatte iku deshou.)
  — The temperature will probably continue to drop from now on.
⑩ これからも 日本語を 勉強していきたいです。
  (Korekara mo nihongo o benkyou shite ikitai desu.)
  — I want to continue studying Japanese from now on.

conversation.title: This cake looks delicious!
conversation.title_ja: このケーキ、おいしそうですね

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: わあ、このケーキ、おいしそうですね。
lines.romaji: Waa, kono keeki, oishisou desu ne.

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: ええ。最近 この店は 人気が 出てきましたよ。
lines.romaji: Ee. Saikin kono mise wa ninki ga dete kimashita yo.
lines.english: Yes. This shop has been gaining popularity recently.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: あ、外を 見てください。雨が 降りそうですね。
lines.romaji: A, soto o mite kudasai. Ame ga furisou desu ne.
lines.english: Oh, look outside. It looks like it's going to rain.

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: そうですね。空が だんだん 暗く なってきましたね。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ne. Sora ga dandan kuraku natte kimashita ne.
lines.english: You're right. The sky has been getting gradually darker.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: 傘を 持ってきませんでした。どうしよう。
lines.romaji: Kasa o motte kimasen deshita. Doushiyou.
lines.english: I didn't bring an umbrella. What shall I do?

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: 大丈夫ですよ。コンビニで 買っていきましょう。
lines.romaji: Daijoubu desu yo. Konbini de katte ikimashou.
lines.english: It's fine. Let's buy one at the convenience store on the way.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: そうしましょう。じゃ、そろそろ 出ましょうか。
lines.romaji: Sou shimashou. Ja, sorosoro demashou ka.
lines.english: Let's do that. Well, shall we leave soon?
